label: Op. Litani 1978 — IDF Advance
notes: IDF 25,000 troops advance from Israel border to Litani River Mar 1978; 1,100+ killed, 250,000 displaced; UNIFIL established

label: Op. Peace for Galilee — IDF Reaches Beirut (1982)
notes: IDF 76,000 troops advance from Israeli border to Beirut, besieging city for 10 weeks; 17,000–20,000 Lebanese killed

label: PLO Evacuation from Beirut (Aug–Sep 1982)
notes: 8,000–14,000 PLO fighters evacuate Beirut under international supervision to Tunisia and other Arab states after IDF siege; MNF supervises

label: Syrian Army Enters Lebanon (1976)
notes: Syrian forces enter Lebanon Jun 1976 to restrain PLO; Syrian occupation persists 1976–2005; 30,000+ troops at peak

label: Syrian Troop Withdrawal (Apr 2005)
notes: Syrian forces complete withdrawal Apr 26, 2005 following Cedar Revolution and Hariri assassination; ends 29-year occupation

label: Israeli Airstrikes on Dahiyeh 2006
damage: Beirut southern suburbs heavily damaged; 7,000 sorties total in 34-day war

label: Hezbollah Rockets into Northern Israel 2006
damage: 59 Israelis killed (mostly civilians); Haifa, Kiryat Shmona, and 50+ towns hit; $1.2B Israeli damage
notes: Hezbollah fires 4,228 rockets into northern Israel during 34-day war; Haifa struck for first time since 1948

label: IDF Strike Kills Nasrallah — Dahiyeh (Sep 27, 2024)
damage: Nasrallah's underground command bunker destroyed; multi-story building collapsed
notes: F-35s drop 2,000-lb bunker-buster bombs; Nasrallah buried under rubble; IDF announces elimination Sep 28

label: IDF Ground Ops in South Lebanon (Oct–Nov 2024)
notes: IDF 98th Division crosses Blue Line Oct 1, 2024; targets Hezbollah tunnels and infrastructure in border villages including Khiyam, Ayta ash-Sha'b, Bint Jbeil

label: Hezbollah Rocket Salvos into Israel (2024)
damage: Continued rocket attacks on northern Israel Oct–Nov 2024 before ceasefire; some penetrated to Haifa area
notes: Hezbollah continues rocket fire despite Nasrallah death; leadership disrupted by pager attacks

label: UNIFIL Blue Line (Lebanon-Israel Border)
notes: UN-demarcated Blue Line separating Lebanon and Israel; UNIFIL 10,500 troops patrol; IDF crossed Oct 2024, partially withdrew by early 2025

label: Iranian Arms Supply Route to Hezbollah
notes: Iran-Syria-Hezbollah weapons corridor through Bekaa Valley; used to transfer advanced missiles, precision guidance systems; targeted by Israeli strikes 2013–2024

label: Israeli Airstrikes on Baalbek (Oct 2024)
damage: Multiple strikes on Baalbek city and surroundings targeting Hezbollah infrastructure; civilian area impacts
casualties: Dozens killed in Bekaa Valley strikes
notes: First time Baalbek city struck in modern conflict; UNESCO World Heritage site proximity concerns raised

label: US Multinational Force — Beirut Airport (1982–84)
notes: US Marines, French paratroopers, Italian and UK forces deploy to Beirut Airport 1982; Oct 23, 1983 barracks bombing kills 241 Americans; MNF withdraws 1984

label: Lebanese Army Deploys South (2025)
notes: Lebanese Armed Forces deploy to southern Lebanon under Nov 2024 ceasefire terms and UNSCR 1701; first significant army presence in the south since 1969
